data_notes: Used combined_rollups.json windows['7'] and windows['28'] as authoritative for recent load totals.
Sleep data are missing for the last 7-day window (sleep_days_with_data = 0), so last7 sleep/HRV/RHR trends cannot be computed from provided signals.
Forecast readiness/overreach is treated as authoritative for readiness.status as instructed.

Training-load values in the deterministic forecast may be computed with a different internal method than the rollup-reported training_load_hours; plan decisions are driven by the provided forecast status + rollup context.
Readiness status was set from deterministic readiness_and_risk_forecast.json.
Guardrails: enforced ramp rate (max_ramp_pct=10.0%). last7_hours=10.175, allowed_hours=11.193, planned_hours=6.300.

days.purpose: Reduce accumulated fatigue and lower overreach risk before adding any structured work.
days.target_intensity: Rest (no training). Optional 15–25 min easy mobility only.
days.title: Full rest + mobility reset
days.workout: No training session. Optional: 10–15 min gentle hip/ankle mobility + 5–10 min easy walk if you feel stiff.

days.purpose: Maintain aerobic frequency while prioritizing recovery (low musculoskeletal cost vs running).
days.terrain: Flat to rolling; avoid long climbs.
days.title: Easy aerobic ride (keep it conversational)
days.workout: 60 min easy spin. If legs feel heavy, keep cadence smooth and cap effort well below the HR you’ve been riding at recently.

days.purpose: Keep run economy and routine without adding major stress during a fatigued phase.
days.target_intensity: Easy; finish feeling like you could keep going.
days.terrain: Flat/soft surface if possible; avoid steep descents.
days.title: Easy run (short and relaxed)
days.workout: 40 min easy run. Optional: 4 x 15–20 sec relaxed strides ONLY if you feel fresh (full easy jogging between).

days.purpose: Aerobic support and leg turnover without turning it into another high-load day.
days.target_intensity: Easy-to-moderate aerobic (still controlled; no threshold work).
days.terrain: Rolling is fine; keep climbs very controlled.
days.title: Aerobic ride with light cadence play
days.workout: 75 min aerobic. Include 6 x 1 min high-cadence spin-ups (light gear) with 2 min easy between—keep effort low, focus on smoothness.

days.purpose: One small quality stimulus to retain fitness while overall volume is reduced due to fatigue/high risk.
days.target_intensity: Tempo-lite: steady/controlled (comfortably hard, not a race).
days.terrain: Flat to gently rolling; avoid steep grades.
days.title: Controlled quality: short tempo (only if feeling stable)
days.workout: 10 min easy warm-up + 3 x 6 min steady tempo (controlled) with 3 min easy between + 8–10 min cool-down. If you feel unusually heavy/tired, replace with 45–55 min easy instead (no intensity).

days.purpose: Maintain trail specificity and durable aerobic base while keeping intensity low (long ≠ hard).
days.target_intensity: Long easy aerobic; hike the steeps; keep effort capped.
days.title: Long easy trail session (aerobic, not hard)
days.workout: 1h45 easy trail run/hike. Keep descents controlled and fueling consistent (start fueling early). If legs are still cooked, shorten to 75–90 min.

days.purpose: Facilitate recovery from the long trail day and set up a better next block.
days.target_intensity: Very easy recovery (keep it truly easy).
days.title: Recovery ride or easy walk (flush)
days.workout: 45 min very easy spin OR 45 min easy walk if you prefer. No intervals, no hills.

readiness.rationale: Deterministic readiness is fatigued with high overreach risk. Recent 7d training load is flagged as elevated vs your recent rolling baseline, and we also have no sleep data in the last 7 days, so we’ll bias this week toward a deload (reduced volume, minimal intensity) while keeping frequency and one controlled quality touch.

actions: Sleep focus: aim to match your established baseline sleep habits (28d mean sleep is available, but last 7d has no sleep recordings—prioritize consistency).
Fueling: for the long trail day, start carbohydrates early and hydrate steadily; keep post-session protein + carbs within 1–2 hours.
Mobility: 10–15 min most days (calves/feet/hips), plus light eccentric calf/quad work only if soreness is low.
Easy intensity discipline: keep most sessions clearly below the HRs you’ve been averaging on recent rides/runs to reduce cumulative load.

risks.message: Overreach risk is high and readiness is fatigued; prioritize deload volume and avoid stacking intensity. This plan keeps volume well below your last-7 moving time and includes only one controlled hard session.
No sleep data in the last 7 days reduces confidence in recovery assessment; keep intensity optional and err on the side of easy if you feel off.

snapshot.notes: Last 7d load is bike-heavy with one big vertical TrailRun (21.02 km / 1621 m+ on 2026-03-07) plus two higher-HR rides (2026-03-09 avgHR 157; 2026-03-10 avgHR 147). No sleep data recorded in the last 7 days, limiting recovery visibility.
